MELItem.Title: Center Tank Fuel Override/Jettison Pump Systems

ListItem.Para: Inoperative pump is deactivated.
Fuel scavenge system operates normally.
Crossfeed valve operates normally.
Center tank quantity indication operates normally.

Note.Para: The associated fuel pump motor controller may be removed provided the dispatch tool K24002 is installed.

ListItem.Para: For left center tank pump inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker OVRD JETT PUMP CTRL-C L and lock with an INOP tag.
For right center tank pump inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker OVRD JETT PUMP CTRL-C R and lock with an INOP tag.
For center tank fuel:
When operative center tank pump switch is on, set crossfeed switch on.
When operative center tank pump switch is off, set crossfeed switch off.
Pumps are deactivated.
Fuel scavenge system operates normally.
Center tank quantity indication operates normally.
For center tank fuel, fuel quantity remaining in main tanks is adequate to reach a suitable airport if scavenge system fails at any time.
Center tank fuel is accounted for in the airplane weight and balance in the event center tank fuel cannot be used.

Note.Para: The associated fuel pump motor controller may be removed provided the dispatch tool K24002 is installed.

ListItem.Para: Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker OVRD JETT PUMP CTRL-C L and lock with an INOP tag.
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker OVRD JETT PUMP CTRL-C R and lock with an INOP tag.
For center tank fuel:

Note.Para: The FUEL IN CENTER advisory message may be displayed.

ListItem.Para: For center tank fuel required, ensure fuel quantity in main tanks is adequate to reach a suitable airport if scavenge system fails at any time.
Reduce the maximum zero fuel weight by the amount of the center tank fuel.
Adjust the zero fuel weight center of gravity forward limit based on the amount of center tank fuel.
Inoperative pump is deactivated.
Crossfeed valve operates normally.
Center tank quantity indication operates normally.
For center tank fuel, fuel quantity in main tanks is adequate to reach a suitable airport if remaining center pump fails at any time.
Center tank fuel is accounted for in the airplane weight and balance in the event center tank fuel cannot be used.

Note.Para: The associated fuel pump motor controller may be removed provided the dispatch tool K24002 is installed.

ListItem.Para: For left center tank pump inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker OVRD JETT PUMP CTRL-C L and lock with an INOP tag.
For right center tank pump inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker OVRD JETT PUMP CTRL-C R and lock with an INOP tag.
For center tank fuel:
For center tank fuel required, ensure fuel quantity in main tanks is adequate to reach a suitable airport if remaining center pump fails at any time.
Reduce the maximum zero fuel weight by the amount of the center tank fuel.
Adjust the zero fuel weight center of gravity forward limit based on the amount of center tank fuel.
When operative center tank pump switch is on, set crossfeed switch on.
When operative center tank pump switch is off, set crossfeed switch off.
Pumps are deactivated.
Center tank quantity indication operates normally.
Center tank fuel is considered unusable and is accounted for in the airplane weight and balance.

Note.Para: AFM fuel loading and usage limitations are for usable fuel.
The associated fuel pump motor controller may be removed provided the dispatch tool K24002 is installed.

ListItem.Para: Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker OVRD JETT PUMP CTRL-C L and lock with an INOP tag.
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker OVRD JETT PUMP CTRL-C R and lock with an INOP tag.
For center tank fuel:

Note.Para: Center tank fuel is considered unusable.
The FUEL IN CENTER advisory message may be displayed.
FUEL UNUSABLE CTR will be displayed after takeoff when dispatched with trapped fuel in center tank.

ListItem.Para: Reduce the maximum zero fuel weight by the amount of the center tank fuel.
Adjust the zero fuel weight center of gravity forward limit based on the amount of center tank fuel.
Add center tank fuel quantity to the RESERVES on the FMC PERF INIT page prior to flight.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One or both may be inoperative.
One or both may be inoperative.
